codeReview.summary: The feature adds a Rust workspace manager and routes the frontend store through native commands, but it does not fully satisfy the required native lifecycle/event ownership. The new backend event surface is emitted but never consumed, recent-workspace persistence still depends on frontend localStorage for restore/clear behavior, and invalid refresh failures do not surface visible user feedback even though the feature contract requires it.

issues.description: Only invalid open failures show visible feedback. When workspace_refresh fails, refreshTree() returns quietly without surfacing result.message, so a broken/inaccessible workspace refresh does not provide the visible failure feedback required by the feature contract.

Restore-on-launch and close-folder persistence are still frontend-owned through localStorage (volt.currentProject). The Rust workspace manager only stores in-memory recentProjects and active_root_path, so the claimed native ownership of lifecycle persistence handling is not actually implemented in this feature.

The backend emits workspace://lifecycle events, but no frontend code listens to them. This leaves the advertised native command/event surface effectively unused and means lifecycle state transitions are still driven entirely by direct command responses and local store logic.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The native-core-worker skill says user-visible behavior changes should use the agent-browser when the local app surface is reachable, but the transcript contains no browser/manual UI verification and the handoff only reports command-level validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file .factory/skills/native-core-worker/SKILL.md section "Verify at three levels" requires manual/user-surface verification when behavior changes are visible. Transcript skeleton for worker session c1b740e7-557f-407f-b37f-cb624ed31c35 shows only Read/Grep/ApplyPatch/Execute validation steps and no agent-browser invocation or interactiveChecks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Workspace lifecycle now spans a native manager plus frontend localStorage restore semantics, but .factory/library has no note documenting that persistence is still partially frontend-owned after this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library is empty, while src/lib/shared/stores/project.svelte.ts lines 449-457 and 543 still read/write volt.currentProject from localStorage despite the new Rust workspace manager in src-tauri/src/domains/workspace/manager.rs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No shared-state/service documentation records the new native workspace commands or emitted workspace lifecycle event channel.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml only lists high-level repo commands/services and .factory/library is empty, while src-tauri/src/lib.rs registers workspace_get_state/workspace_open/workspace_refresh/workspace_close/workspace_replace_recent_projects and src-tauri/src/domains/workspace/manager.rs emits workspace://lifecycle.

summary: Review failed: the feature moves some workspace orchestration into Rust, but native lifecycle ownership is incomplete. Persistence handling remains frontend-owned via localStorage, refresh failures lack required visible feedback, and the new workspace event surface is emitted but unused by the frontend.
